# Personal Preferences
<!-- generated by agentic-stack onboard v0.6.0 on 2026-04-18T11:53:37 -->
<!-- re-run:  agentic-stack <harness> --reconfigure  to update -->

> **This file is yours.** Edit it any time — it's the first thing your AI
> reads at the start of every session.

## Code style
- Language(s): Go (primary), TypeScript (Playwright e2e), templ (HTML templating)
- Explanations: concise — short sentences, no narration of internal thought
- Comments: default to none; only when the "why" is non-obvious

## Workflow
- Test strategy: test-after (Playwright e2e for UI, Go tests where it matters)
- Commit style: conventional commits (`feat:`, `fix:`, `refactor:`, `test:`, `style:`, `docs:`)
- Branching: feature branches merged to `main` via PR
- Before editing any symbol: run `gitnexus_impact` first (see CLAUDE.md)
- Before committing: run `gitnexus_detect_changes()` to verify scope

## Communication
- Review depth: critical issues only
- Tone: direct, skip pleasantries
- Surface tradeoffs: always
- Confirm before risky/destructive actions (force push, rm -rf, overwriting uncommitted work)

## Stack
- Backend: Go 1.26 (bitmerchant module), Watermill (CQRS/event bus), AWS SDK v2
- Frontend: templ + tailwind-merge-go, server-rendered HTML
- Arch pattern: CQRS/DDD/Clean Architecture (ThreeDotsLabs style — use `/threedots audit` skill)
- Tasks: Taskfile.yml
- Git hooks: lefthook
- Deploy: Dockerfile + docker-compose.yml

## Constraints
- Follow CQRS/DDD boundaries — per-context ports and services (recent refactor in b67125a…)
- Don't bypass lefthook or git hooks (`--no-verify`) unless explicitly asked
- Don't skip `gitnexus_impact` on symbol edits
- Don't mock DBs in integration tests — hit a real one
- Keep commits small and conventional; prefer new commits over `--amend`

## Off-limits
- Never force-push to `main`
- Never delete `.gitnexus/` or `.agent/memory/episodic/` — archive instead
- Never modify `.agent/protocols/permissions.md`
